diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
+import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge";
+import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
+import { VariantProps, cva } from "class-variance-authority";
+
+const badgeVariants = cva(
+    "inline-flex items-center rounded-full font-medium transition-colors focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-ring focus:ring-offset-2",
+    {
+        variants: {
+            status: {
+                success:
+                    "bg-green-100 text-green-700 hover:bg-green-100/80 dark:bg-green-500/10 dark:text-green-400",
+                warning:
+                    "bg-orange-100 text-orange-700 hover:bg-orange-100/80 dark:bg-orange-500/10 dark:text-orange-400",
+                destructive:
+                    "bg-red-100 text-red-700 hover:bg-red-100/80 dark:bg-red-500/10 dark:text-red-400",
+                info: "bg-blue-100 text-blue-700 hover:bg-blue-100/80 dark:bg-blue-500/10 dark:text-blue-400",
+                default:
+                    "bg-gray-100 text-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-100/80 dark:bg-gray-800 dark:text-gray-300",
+            },
+            size: {
+                sm: "px-2 py-0.5 text-xs",
+                md: "px-2.5 py-0.5 text-sm",
+                lg: "px-3 py-1 text-base",
+            },
+            dot: {
+                true: "pl-1.5",
+                false: "",
+            },
+        },
+        defaultVariants: {
+            status: "default",
+            size: "sm",
+            dot: false,
+        },
+    }
+);
+
+interface StatusBadgeProps
+    extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>,
+    VariantProps<typeof badgeVariants> {
+    label: string;
+}
+
+export function StatusBadge({
+    className,
+    status,
+    size,
+    dot = true,
+    label,
+    ...props
body:
🟡 **StatusBadge dot has no background color when `status` prop is omitted** When the `status` prop is not explicitly passed to `StatusBadge`, it remains `undefined` (from `VariantProps`). The CVA `defaultVariants` correctly applies `"default"` styling to the outer container, but the dot color logic at lines 61–65 compares the raw `status` variable (which is `undefined`) against string literals like `"default"`. None of the conditions match, so the dot `` renders with no `bg-*` class — producing an invisible circle.
Root Cause and Impact CVA's `defaultVariants` only affect the class string output of `badgeVariants()` — they do not change the actual value of the `status` variable in JavaScript. So when a caller writes ``, the `status` destructured prop is `undefined`, not `"default"`. The dot color logic: ```tsx status === "success" && "bg-green-500", status === "warning" && "bg-orange-500", status === "destructive" && "bg-red-500", status === "info" && "bg-blue-500", status === "default" && "bg-gray-500" ``` All evaluate to `false` because `undefined !== "default"`. The dot renders as an empty 1.5×1.5 `` with no background color. **Impact:** Any `StatusBadge` usage that omits the `status` prop (relying on the default) will show an invisible dot indicator, which is visually broken.
```suggestion export function StatusBadge({ className, status = "default", size, dot = true, label, ...props }: StatusBadgeProps) { ``` --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

🚩 **FilterBar manages its own search state, potentially conflicting with parent control** The `FilterBar` at `components/shared/filter-bar.tsx:32` manages `searchValue` via internal `useState`, while also calling `onSearchChange` to notify the parent. This makes the search input an uncontrolled-to-parent component — the parent cannot reset or externally set the search value (e.g., when clearing all filters via `onClearAll`). If a parent calls `onClearAll` and expects the search to also reset, it won't — the internal state at line 32 is never cleared externally. This is a design limitation rather than a bug, but worth noting if `onClearAll` is expected to reset search too. --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*
